templates: Give me a title—or a genre + decade. That’s enough.

Good to have you here. The first signal decides the lane.

No stall. We recover with a single clean cue.

rules: If Marion returns a non-empty reply, that reply wins.
Packets may shape tone, chips, and lane hints, but may not replace Marion authority.
Fallback packets may run only when backend reply is empty, missing, or hard-failed.
Error packets may run only on transport, parser, or contract failure.
Greeting and prompt packets may assist orientation, but never override a live Marion reply.
Specific 40D greeting packets may classify tone and suggest an opening response, but must not override a valid Marion final reply.
Greeting packets must answer once and move forward; they must not become loop fallbacks.

runtimeContract.greetingIntentRule: Specific 40D greeting packets should be evaluated before broad greeting packets; they classify tone/energy and then yield to Marion final authority.
runtimeContract.micTextAlignmentRule: Speech final transcript must be normalized and sent through the same submit path as typed text before packet matching.

normalization.inputRule: Normalize assistant-name voice/text aliases before packet matching. Mic and text must share the same submit path before Marion routing.
